title: Malaxis micholitziana
taxonomy: Malaxis micholitziana is classified in the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants in the world. intro: Malaxis micholitziana, also known as Micholitz's orchid or Chinese adder's tongue, is a species of orchid that belongs to the genus Malaxis. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating various ailments such as respiratory issues and skin conditions. Bioactive constituents of Malaxis micholitziana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Malaxis micholitziana is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and capsules, to promote respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and coughs.
